hyperedges.label: Auth flow: register → login → refresh (rotate) → logout
register transaction flow (company + first-admin user + token pair)
browser auth flow (register page -> AuthForm -> useRegister -> dashboard)
test gate (e2e spec + setup.env + db helpers + compose postgres-test)
shared auth schemas single source of truth (api + web)

nodes.label: Phase 0: Foundation (COMPLETE)
nodes.rationale: Built pnpm+Turborepo monorepo, apps/api (NestJS 11) + apps/web (Next.js 16) skeletons, packages/shared Zod SSoT, docker-compose (postgres:16+redis:7), GitHub Actions CI, root ESLint gate. Adopted current stable majors via ADR 0001 rather than the plan's 2024-era pins because shipping mid-2026 on stale majors would be a reviewer red flag and force painful mid-project upgrades.

nodes.label: Phase 1a: Auth Backend (COMPLETE)
nodes.rationale: Implemented register/login/refresh/logout/me + admin-probe against real Postgres+Redis. Single 0001_init migration with all 12 domain models to reduce migration churn across later phases. TokenService is the only place that signs/verifies JWT and touches the Redis denylist, so there is a single verify source.

nodes.label: Phase 1b: Auth Frontend (COMPLETE)
nodes.rationale: Built login/register pages (RHF+Zod shared schemas), Zustand in-memory token store, TanStack Query mutations + silent refresh on 401, (dashboard) Server Component guard. UI primitives hand-rolled because shadcn/ui init is known-broken on Tailwind v4 (spec §7/§10).

nodes.label: Refresh-token rotation on every refresh
nodes.rationale: Every POST /auth/refresh issues a fresh access+refresh pair with new jtis; the consumed jti is added to a Redis denylist (auth:denylist:{jti}) with TTL equal to the token's remaining lifetime so entries auto-expire. Reuse of a revoked refresh returns 401, signalling theft; rotation bounds a stolen refresh token's usefulness to a single use.

nodes.label: httpOnly sameSite=lax refresh cookie
nodes.rationale: Refresh token stored in an httpOnly, sameSite=lax cookie named refresh_token (secure:true in prod) so it survives page reloads without being readable by JavaScript, avoiding the XSS exposure of localStorage. Browser sends it automatically on /auth/refresh and /auth/logout with credentials:include.

nodes.label: Access token held in memory only (Zustand)
nodes.rationale: Access token is held in memory on the client (Zustand), never in localStorage/sessionStorage/document.cookie. Verified statelessly (signature+expiry only, no Redis lookup). Lost on reload, which triggers a silent refresh via the cookie to repopulate {user, token} via /me.
nodes.norm_label: access token held in memory only (zustand)

nodes.label: Access-token statelessness preserved
nodes.rationale: TokenService.verify skips the Redis denylist for typ:access. An access denylist would negate stateless verification (spec §4); the short access TTL (default 15m) is the accepted exposure window for a stolen access token.

nodes.label: Redis denylist keyed by jti
nodes.rationale: A revocation list keyed only by user cannot distinguish two valid refresh tokens of the same user. Adding a jti claim and keying the denylist by jti enables per-token logout/rotation. This is correctly a hybrid model (signed tokens + server-side denylist), not 'stateless'.

nodes.label: typ claim enforcement
nodes.rationale: TokenService.verify(token, typ) rejects a token whose typ claim does not match the expected type, so an access token cannot be used as a refresh token and vice-versa.

nodes.label: First-admin bootstrap via register
nodes.rationale: POST /auth/register accepts a company name and transactionally creates Company + first User(role:admin) (spec §3.2). The User.email unique constraint is the source of truth for duplicate detection; a race that slips the pre-check surfaces as Prisma P2002 mapped to 409. An earlier companyId-based variant was tried and reverted to match the spec contract.

nodes.label: Logout is idempotent
nodes.rationale: Invalid/missing refresh on logout returns 200 {success:true} as a no-op so flaky client retries are harmless. This is a deliberate, documented deviation from spec §4's '401 if no valid refresh' on logout.

nodes.label: 11 critical-path tests (TDD, real Postgres)
nodes.rationale: Critical-path tests are written within the phase that implements the feature (TDD), not deferred to Phase 9, because 'tests later' implies 'no tests' and contradicts path-based coverage intent. Tests run on real PostgreSQL, never SQLite, because enum/JSON/@updatedAt semantics diverge and produce false-green tests. 11/11 pass: register(tx Company+admin), dup-email 409, weak-password 400, login valid, login 401, throttle 429, refresh rotation, revoked-jti 401, me, RolesGuard admin-probe 200/403, logout revokes.

nodes.label: Testing inside each phase (not deferred to Phase 9)
nodes.rationale: Original plan put all tests in Phase 9. Problem: 'tests later' equals 'no tests', contradicting TDD and path-based coverage. Change: critical-path tests written within the implementing phase (TDD red-green-refactor); Phase 9 renamed E2E + Polish carrying only Playwright, Swagger, error/loading states.
nodes.norm_label: testing inside each phase (not deferred to phase 9)

nodes.label: @nestjs/throttler on /auth/login + /auth/register
nodes.rationale: Redis 'rate-limit counter' was mentioned in the stack but never scheduled in a phase. Brute-force protection is infrastructure, not a feature, so @nestjs/throttler (10 req/min/IP) is added to login/register in Phase 1. In-memory storage for Phase 1 (single instance); Redis-backed storage deferred to Phase 8/9 when multi-instance matters.

nodes.label: Dev proxy: /api/* → API_ORIGIN (default :4000)
nodes.rationale: Next.js rewrite proxies /api/:path* to ${API_ORIGIN}/:path* so the browser issues same-origin requests and the sameSite=lax refresh cookie is sent/accepted in dev without cross-origin CORS-cookie gymnastics or wildcard-origin-with-credentials rejection. CORS already permits localhost:3000 with credentials:true. Prod cross-origin + sameSite:none;secure deferred to Phase 10.

nodes.label: Refresh cookie path widened /auth → /
nodes.rationale: The (dashboard) Server Component guard must read the refresh cookie at /dashboard via next/headers cookies(), so the cookie cannot be scoped to /auth. Widening to / is safe: it is httpOnly (no JS access) and only /auth/refresh + /auth/logout consume it server-side; the frontend reaches them via the /api same-origin proxy.

nodes.label: (dashboard) Server Component cookie guard
nodes.rationale: The guard checks cookie presence, not validity: presence means 'the browser might have a session' (the access token is in-memory and never reaches the server); validity is re-established client-side via silent refresh. A revoked-but-present cookie falls through the guard, but the dashboard's failed-refresh path redirects to /login.

nodes.label: Zod→400 via per-route ZodValidationPipe
nodes.rationale: Chose per-route ZodValidationPipe (→400 on weak password) instead of the plan's global ZodExceptionFilter. Same spec outcome, co-located with the route it validates.

hyperedges.label: Auth flow: register → login → refresh (rotate) → logout
register transaction flow (company + first-admin user + token pair)
browser auth flow (register page -> AuthForm -> useRegister -> dashboard)
test gate (e2e spec + setup.env + db helpers + compose postgres-test)
shared auth schemas single source of truth (api + web)
